case_study.title: Flurizan (tarenflurbil) — Alzheimer's Phase 3 efficacy miss
case_study.framing: Historical educational case study. Not a recommendation to resurrect the asset or treat patients.

program.name: Flurizan AD program
program.moa: Gamma-secretase modulator (NSAID-derived); intended to reduce amyloidogenic Aβ42 processing
program.cou_note: Educational GapForge case study — hypotheses for scientific discussion only
program.stall_summary: Phase 3 trial in mild AD failed co-primary cognitive and functional endpoints; development discontinued.

trials.outcome_summary: No statistically significant efficacy vs placebo on co-primary endpoints in mild AD population; program discontinued.

hypotheses.claim: Clinical endpoints (ADAS-Cog / ADCS-ADL) may have been insufficiently sensitive to detect a modest disease-modifying effect in the enrolled mild AD population over the studied duration.
hypotheses.suggested_experiment: Re-analyse public AD trial datasets for endpoint sensitivity and power under modest effect sizes; pre-specify mechanistic PD biomarkers as co-primary or key secondary in future designs.

literature_refs.title: Translational lessons from Flurizan failure in AD
literature_refs.note: Discusses Phase 3 miss and stratification/endpoint considerations

hypotheses.claim: Enrichment based on clinical mildness (e.g. MMSE bands) rather than mechanistic pathology markers may have diluted a true biological responder subgroup.
hypotheses.suggested_experiment: Map published amyloid/tau biomarker distributions in contemporary mild AD cohorts against historical Flurizan inclusion criteria; quantify dilution risk for Aβ-pathway agents.

literature_refs.title: Translational lessons from Flurizan failure in AD
literature_refs.note: Post-hoc mild subgroup thinking and biomarker stratification caveats

hypotheses.claim: Central target engagement or Aβ42 modulation may have been inadequate at clinically tolerated exposures to produce a measurable clinical benefit.
hypotheses.suggested_experiment: Assemble public exposure–response and CSF biomarker reports for γ-secretase modulators; define a minimum PD effect size linked to clinical change before any similar MoA is reconsidered.

literature_refs.title: ClinicalTrials.gov tarenflurbil records
literature_refs.note: Registry context for completed efficacy studies
